Title: IWS - Verify LED configuration persists through deploy cycle
2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
4. Configure the LED ring to color blue, brightness 50%.
Preconditions: 1. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).

Title: 2. Verify the LED configuration is active on the device.
3. Disconnect from the Core (F7).
4. Emulate the design (F6).
5. Change the LED ring to color red, brightness 100%.
6. Disconnect from emulation (F7).
7. Deploy the design to the Core again (F5).
8. Verify the LED configuration on the device.
Preconditions: 1. After step 8, the LED ring is set to red at 100% brightness.

Title: 2. The values match those set in emulation (step 5), not the original values (step 4).
3. The physical device LED ring reflects the updated configuration.
IWS - Verify detection zone settings persist through deploy cycle
2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
4. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).
5. Configure detection zone sensitivity to 50% and range to Medium via the device Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Verify detection zone settings are active on the device.

Title: 2. Open the device Web UI and change detection zone sensitivity to 80% and range to High.
3. Disconnect from the Core (F7).
4. Deploy the design to the Core again (F5).
5. Verify detection zone settings on the device.
Preconditions: 1. After step 5, sensitivity is 80% and range is High.

Title: 2. The values match those set in QPM (step 2), not the original values.
3. Design deployment does not overwrite detection zone settings configured via the device Web UI.
IWS - Verify component properties persist through deploy cycle
2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
4. Set component name to "Lobby Sensor" and temperature unit to °C.
Preconditions: 1. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).

Title: 2. Verify component properties are active.
3. Disconnect from the Core (F7).
4. Emulate the design (F6).
5. Change the component name to "Conference Room" and temperature unit to °F.
6. Disconnect from emulation (F7).
7. Deploy the design to the Core again (F5).
8. Verify component properties.
Preconditions: 1. After step 8, the component name is "Conference Room" and temperature unit is °F.

Title: 2. The values match those set in emulation (step 5), not the original values (step 4).
3. The deployed design reflects all updated properties.
